---
title: "Emergency Room Care Across the United States"
subtitle: "INFO 526 Data Analysis and Visualization"
author: "Michael Burris"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: images/hospital_background.jpg
  data-background-size: stretch
  data-background-opacity: "0.7"
  data-slide-number: none
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:  ['data/customtheming.scss']
  
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup, load packages, and load data
#| include: false

# Plot theme
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11))

# For better figure resolution
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.retina = 3, 
  dpi = 300, 
  fig.width = 6, 
  fig.asp = 0.618 
  )

# Load packages
#install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(tidyverse,
               scales,
               ggthemes
               )

# Load dataset
er_data <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-04-08/care_state.csv")

# All references can be found in project proposal
```

## Focus Areas

- Mental Health Patient Treatment

  - Visit duration

  - Differences between states/regions

- Early Departures

  - Percentage of patients by state who left early

  - If population has an influence

## Overarching Questions

1.  Which states/regions are the most and least effective time wise at treating psychiatric/mental health patients that are admitted to the emergency room?
2.  Does state population have an effect on what percent of patients leave the emergency room without being seen?

## Mental Health Patient Treatment

```{r, warning=FALSE, fig.width = 7}
#| label: Mental health patient treatment

# Filter data to only include emergency room observations, patients that are non psyciatric/mental health patients, and only states not federal districts or territories.
patients <- er_data |>
  filter(condition == "Emergency Department", 
         measure_id == "OP_18c", state != "DC", state != "AS", state != "GU",
         state != "MP", state != "PR", state != "VI")

# Create data frame that only includes the 5 states with the longest ER visit times for mental health patients
longest_visits <- patients |>
  slice_max(score, n = 5)

# Create a data frame that only includes the 5 states with the shortest ER visit times for mental health patients
shortest_visits <- patients |>
  slice_min(score, n = 5)

# Join the two new data frames together
p_data = longest_visits |>
  full_join(shortest_visits)

# Create a new column that spells out the state names for the states with the longest and shortest ER visit times for mental health patients
p_data = p_data |>
  mutate(
    state_full = case_when(
      state %in% c("DE") ~ "Delaware", state %in% c("KS") ~ "Kansas",
      state %in% c("MA") ~ "Massachusetts", state %in% c("MD") ~ "Maryland",
      state %in% c("MT") ~ "Montana", state %in% c("ND") ~ "North Dakota",
      state %in% c("NE") ~ "Nebraska", state %in% c("NY") ~ "New York",
      state %in% c("RI") ~ "Rhode Island", state %in% c("SD") ~ "South Dakota"
    )
  )

# Plot my data
ggplot(p_data, aes(x = score, y = factor(state_full, levels = rev(levels(factor(state_full)))))) + 
  geom_point(size = 4) + 
  labs(
    title = "Highest & Lowest Median ER Visit Times",
    subtitle = "Psychiatric/mental health patients",
    x = "Minutes",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services | TidyTuesday"
  ) + 
  theme(
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "cornsilk4"),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "cornsilk4")
  )
```

## Mental Health Patient Treatment (cont.)

```{r, warning=FALSE, fig.width = 7}
#| label: Mental Health Patient Treatment (cont.)

# Modify my data to include region for my 10 previously selected states
p_data <- p_data |>
  mutate(
    region = case_when(
      state %in% c("DE", "MD") ~ "South",
      state %in% c("KS", "ND", "NE", "SD") ~ "Midwest",
      state %in% c("MT") ~ "West",
      TRUE ~ "Northeast"
    )
  )

# Plot my data
ggplot(p_data, aes(x = score, y = reorder(state_full, score), fill = region)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  scale_fill_colorblind() + 
  labs(
    title = "Median ER Visit Times by State & Region",
    subtitle = "Psychiatric/mental health patients",
    x = "Minutes",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services | TidyTuesday"
  ) + 
  theme(
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "cornsilk4"),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "cornsilk4")
  )
```

## Early Departures

```{r, warning=FALSE, fig.width = 7}
#| label: Early ER Departures

# Filter data to only include % of patients who left the ER without being seen in each state
p_left <- er_data |>
  filter(condition == "Emergency Department",
         measure_id == "OP_22", state != "DC", state != "AS", state != "GU", 
         state != "MP", state != "PR", state != "VI")

# Load in new state population data
state_populations <- read_csv("data/state_populations.csv")

# Left join
combined_data <- left_join(p_left, state_populations)

# Set my theme
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 14))

# Plot the data
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = score)) + 
         geom_histogram(bins = 5) + 
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) + 
  labs(
    title = "Count of States and their Early ER Departures",
    subtitle = "% left before being seen",
    x = NULL,
    y = "# of states",
    caption = "Source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services | TidyTuesday"
  ) + 
  theme(
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "cornsilk4"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "cornsilk4")
  )

```

## Early Departures (cont.)

```{r, warning=FALSE, fig.width = 7}
#| label: Early ER Departures (cont.)

# Plot the data
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = score, y = population, size = population, color = population)) + 
  #geom_point() + 
  geom_jitter() +
  scale_x_continuous(label = label_percent(scale = 1)) + 
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
  #scale_y_continuous(label = label_number()) + 
  labs(
    title = "Median % Early ER Departures by State",
    caption = "Source: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services | TidyTuesday",
    x = NULL,
    y = "State Population"
  ) + 
  guides(
    title = "State Population",
    size = "none",
    color = "none"
    ) + 
  theme(
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "cornsilk4")
  )
```

## Conclusions

- Mental health patients experience shorter visit times in the Midwest/West regions than they do in the South/Northeast regions

- State population does not have an impact on what percent of patients leave the emergency room before being seen
